approval.note: Do not infer WCAG conformance from automated checks alone. Preserve the public globe, filters and proposal flow while adding exact browser-mode evidence, bounded keyboard-path tests, exact-head review, green CI, Pages deployment and public readback.

metadata.source: Commonworld PR #104 closeout and validator-hardening review follow-up
metadata.observed_limit: The existing browser and validator evidence does not establish forced-colors behaviour, increased-contrast behaviour, complete keyboard-only navigation or visible-focus integrity across the public Commonworld surfaces.

repaired_findings: Reduced-motion CSS used a minimal transition duration, which briefly animated focus-outline width and exposed the prior three-pixel value; transitions are now disabled in reduced-motion mode.
The canonical active-script inventory omitted the new accessibility browser smoke and now records it.
The existing layer-order smoke read its MutationObserver log before the next-frame panel event on CI; it now waits for the observed event before asserting the unchanged ordering invariant.
Two trailing blank lines detected by git diff --check were removed.
The former overlap-only focus predicate accepted a one-pixel intersection; focusable controls that fit must now be fully contained in the layout viewport, with an explicit oversized-control rule.
The former timeout-boundary return could hide late focus settlement; late settlement is now a structured warning after one second and missing containment remains a hard failure after three seconds.
Focus diagnostics now use one explicit plain-object geometry reader, a consistent layout viewport and recorded scroll coordinates.

The existing landscape lane smoke measured a 48-pixel lane during scale(0.96), producing exactly 46.08 pixels in CI; it now waits for the computed scale(1) state before enforcing unchanged size thresholds.

No automatic push workflows had appeared for final merge 7817eca before manual compensation at 18:16Z; exact Pages rebuild and production readback were invoked explicitly and the observed gap is recorded rather than treated as green CI.

local_evidence.operator_review: Final hash-bound diff, CSS cascade, semantic-state assertions and live source were reviewed; no manual screen-reader or physical-device session was performed.

scenarios.forced-colors-public-keyboard: PASS; 18 globe focus targets and 93 text-view focus targets
scenarios.forced-colors-proposal-keyboard: PASS; 25 initial and 27 geographic-form focus targets

final_pages_build.trigger: manual GitHub Pages build API because no automatic merge-sha workflow runs were created

bureau_baseline.doctor_limitation: Pre-existing unrelated lifecycle, queue, lease-scope and legacy evidence findings remain; no T016-specific baseline error was present.

acceptance.forced-colors-surface: PASS in Chromium automation for public and proposal surfaces
acceptance.increased-contrast-surface: PASS in Chromium automation for public and proposal surfaces
acceptance.keyboard-paths: PASS for bounded globe, filter, settings, text-view, catalogue and proposal journeys
acceptance.visible-focus-integrity: PASS for visible outlines, full layout-viewport containment where controls fit, explicit oversized-control spanning, overlay occlusion checks and bounded late-settlement telemetry.
acceptance.semantic-state-preserved: PASS for expanded, checked, pressed, disabled, invalid and focused-error states
acceptance.evidence-bound-closeout: PASS with exact diff, local receipts, CI runs, merge SHA, Pages deployment and byte-equal public CSS readback

explicit_nonclaims: Accessibility acceptance outside the tested Chromium modes, paths and viewport profiles.
Absence of future browser, CDN or client-network regressions.
Existence of an automatic push-triggered validation run for final merge 7817eca; validation is instead bound by identical PR-head and merge trees plus the green PR run.
The one-second late-focus warning threshold as a general product performance SLO.

review_hardening.source: User-provided Playwright review of PR #109 follow-up patch

finding_assessments.reason: The existing diagnostic already copied all DOMRect coordinates into a plain JSON object before crossing the Playwright boundary.
The previous intersection predicate already rejected left=2000 for a 1280-pixel viewport because 2000 < 1280 is false.

finding_assessments.result: Controls that fit must be fully contained within the layout viewport with one-pixel subpixel tolerance; oversized controls must span the viewport on the oversized axis.
A single diagnostic polling path emits machine-readable late-focus warnings after 1000ms and fails hard after 3000ms instead of silently swallowing a timeout.
Browser geometry is read by one explicit page.evaluate diagnostic function; viewport policy and warning classification remain in Node.

finding_assessments.reason: Calling focus() or scrollIntoView() from the test would repair the tested UI path and could hide a real keyboard-focus defect.

pull_requests.purpose: Bound the original focus-settlement wait without weakening the intersection invariant.
Require full viewport containment, expose late settlements, unify diagnostics and wait for stable landscape transforms before geometry assertions.

observed_ci_trigger_gap.compensation: The exact PR-head tree passed GitHub validation and equals the merge tree; the existing Pages source was manually rebuilt for the merge SHA and the repository workflow was manually dispatched for an exact-SHA production readback.

title: Verify forced-colors, increased-contrast and keyboard focus across Commonworld
goal: Make the public globe, discovery filters and Commons proposal path resilient under forced-colors and increased-contrast preferences, and prove that all interactive controls remain keyboard-reachable with visible, unclipped focus and correct semantic state without claiming broader accessibility conformance than the evidence supports.

acceptance.assertion: With forced-colors active, the globe controls, layer and search controls, presence filters, proposal fields, validation messages and primary actions remain visible, distinguishable and operable without relying only on author-defined color.
Where the browser exposes an increased-contrast preference, the public surfaces preserve readable text, boundaries, selected states and focus indicators without clipping, overlap or loss of meaning.

A keyboard-only browser path reaches every public interactive control in logical order, operates disclosures, filters, globe/layer navigation and the proposal form, and encounters no focus trap or unreachable control.
Every keyboard-focusable control has a visible focus indication that is not hidden by overlays, viewport edges, scrolling containers or active-state styling in the tested viewport matrix.
Labels and checked, selected, expanded, disabled, invalid and error states remain programmatically exposed after any CSS or interaction repair.

The closeout records exact browsers, modes and viewport profiles, automated and manual evidence, repaired findings, explicit nonclaims, reviewed diff identity, CI, Pages deployment and public readback.
